name: Comunidades Latinas Unidas En Servicio-Clues
description_short: Minnesota's largest Latino-led nonprofit providing culturally relevant financial counseling, behavioral health, job training, and community support services across five locations.
description_long:
Comunidades Latinas Unidas En Servicio (CLUES) was founded in 1981 as a Latino-led nonprofit organization with a mission to advance social and economic equity and wellbeing for Latino communities in Minnesota. Over 40 years of operation have established CLUES as the state's largest Latino-serving nonprofit, with headquarters in Saint Paul and additional offices in Minneapolis, Willmar, and Austin. The organization operates with a strength-based philosophy, recognizing community assets and empowering individuals to identify and expand upon their capabilities.

CLUES offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services spanning economic prosperity programs, behavioral health clinics, job training and employment support, early childhood initiatives, youth programs, elder services, and survivor advocacy. Their Economic Prosperity program provides financial guidance and resources to help families build stability. The organization also operates the Abriendo Caminos Clinic, recently certified as a Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic (CCBHC), delivering culturally responsive mental health services. Additional offerings include adult education classes, ESL instruction, parenting support, children's tutoring, food resource navigation, and emergency relief assistance.

CLUES distinguishes itself through culturally and linguistically relevant service delivery specifically designed for Latino communities. The organization maintains bilingual staff and materials (English/Spanish), operates a family wellness and childcare business incubator, and has received national recognition including a Blue Ribbon Affiliate award from Parents as Teachers. Their integrated approach addresses multiple life domains simultaneously rather than siloing services, and their leadership pipeline emphasizes Latino community members in decision-making roles.

As a legitimate nonprofit organization with transparent financials available on their website, CLUES provides credible, mission-driven services. However, consumers should note that while they offer economic prosperity and financial guidance programs, they are not specifically a credit repair or debt relief agency. Their strength lies in holistic community development and support rather than specialized credit or lending services. Office hours are limited to weekday business hours (8:30 am–5:00 pm Monday–Friday), which may not accommodate all working families.
pros: Largest Latino-led nonprofit in Minnesota with 40+ years of community trust and track record
Culturally and linguistically responsive services with bilingual staff and materials in English and Spanish
Comprehensive integrated programs addressing economic, behavioral health, employment, and family needs simultaneously
Recently certified as a Certified Community Behavioral Health Clinic (CCBHC) for quality assurance
Blue Ribbon Affiliate designation from Parents as Teachers recognizing evidence-based early childhood services
Multiple convenient locations across Minnesota (St. Paul, Minneapolis, Willmar, Austin)
Transparent financials and governance available on website; legitimate nonprofit with clear mission
cons: Limited office hours (Monday–Friday 8:30 am–5:00 pm only; closed weekends) may not accommodate working families
Not a specialized credit repair or debt relief agency; economic services are advisory rather than dispute-focused
Some resources and guides only available in Spanish, limiting access for English-only speakers
Geographic service area limited to Minnesota; not accessible to consumers in other states
No indication of emergency same-day financial assistance or rapid-access lending products
best_for: Latino families seeking culturally responsive financial guidance and economic stability support in Minnesota
Parents seeking early childhood services, children's tutoring, or parenting education from Spanish-speaking providers
Job seekers and career changers needing employment training and placement assistance with cultural competency
Individuals experiencing domestic violence or sexual assault seeking survivor advocacy and integrated support services
diagnosis: CLUES is best for Latino families and individuals in Minnesota seeking free or low-cost, culturally competent support across multiple life domains—from financial guidance and job training to mental health services and family support. The main caveat is that this is a comprehensive community organization rather than a specialized financial services provider; consumers with primary needs around credit repair, debt settlement, or emergency lending should look elsewhere, though CLUES' economic prosperity programs can provide helpful financial coaching and resource navigation.
services: Economic Prosperity programs and financial guidance
Behavioral health clinics and mental health counseling (CCBHC-certified)
Job training and employment placement services
Adult ESL and English language classes
Early childhood initiatives and parenting education
Survivor Advocacy and Support for domestic violence and sexual assault
Food resource navigation and farmers' market access guides
Family Wellness & Childcare Business Incubator support
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
